grown up.definition: Fully adult; no longer a child.
grown up.example: When children are all grown up, the relationship continues.

progress.example: At first, you make very slow progress with a toddler.

overtake.definition: To go past someone who is in front of you.
overtake.example: One day they will overtake you.

investment.definition: Time, effort, or money put into something to get a good result.
investment.example: All this investment of time and care has been worth it.

reap.definition: To get the results or rewards of your efforts.
reap.example: They reap the benefits of having a secure attachment.

attachment.definition: A strong emotional connection to someone.
attachment.example: A secure attachment style helps them in life.

curiosity.definition: A strong desire to learn and know about things.
curiosity.example: They will have curiosity about the world.

dock.definition: A place where boats come to rest safely (used here as a metaphor for a safe place).
dock.example: They will have a safe dock to return to.

succour.definition: Help and comfort given to someone in difficulty.
succour.example: The safe dock will provide succour and comfort.

mortal.definition: Not living forever; all humans die eventually.
mortal.example: Even though we are mortal, the secure base will remain.

non-intrusively.definition: In a way that does not bother or interfere with someone.
non-intrusively.example: It means a lot when parents take interest non-intrusively.

delight.example: A parent's eyes light up with delight.

beams.example: When a hundred-year-old mother beams in pride.

admiration.definition: A feeling of respect and approval for someone.
admiration.example: Our pride means more than the admiration of others.

praise.definition: Words that show you think someone or something is very good.
praise.example: A parent's pride matters more than praise from others.

triumphs.example: Don't take credit for their triumphs unless they give it to you.

setbacks.example: Don't side-step from your part in their setbacks.

affronted.example: You feel affronted when your child warns you about your choices.

offspring.example: Your adult offspring may have opinions about your life.

lover.definition: A person you are in a romantic relationship with.
lover.example: They warned their parent about their new lover.

inconvenient.example: Listen even when the truth is inconvenient.

swap.example: The roles of parent and child may swap over.

parented.definition: Cared for and looked after like a child.
parented.example: You can find yourself parented by your child.

slip up.example: Parents can slip up once they think their duties are over.

competitive.definition: Wanting to be better or more successful than others.
competitive.example: Parents can feel competitive towards their child.

trump.example: It felt like she was trying to trump me.

flustered.definition: Confused and nervous because of pressure.
flustered.example: She got flustered and changed the subject.

reminded.definition: Made to think about something again.
reminded.example: She was reminded of her own pride in her children.

rivalry.definition: Competition between people who want the same thing.
rivalry.example: Be aware of past habits of rivalry.

rear their heads.definition: To appear again, especially something unwanted.
rear their heads.example: Unhelpful ways of relating can rear their heads again.

dependence.definition: The state of needing someone else for help or support.
dependence.example: Because of past dependence, parents still have influence.

unknowingly.example: Be careful not to unknowingly knock them back.

merged.definition: Combined together so closely that you cannot tell them apart.
merged.example: Don't feel so merged with them that you lose boundaries.

inner critic.definition: The voice inside your head that judges and criticizes you.
inner critic.example: Don't let your inner critic loose on your children.

bonds.definition: Strong connections or ties between people.
bonds.example: Our bonds with our children are some of the most important.

formative.definition: Having a lasting effect on how someone develops.
formative.example: These can be some of the most formative relationships.

influencing.definition: Having an effect on how someone thinks or acts.
influencing.example: See how your childhood is influencing your children's childhood.

fluid.definition: Able to change easily and smoothly.
fluid.example: Being flexible and fluid about roles makes it easier for everyone.

guarantees.definition: Promises that something will definitely happen.

unfair.definition: Not right or equal; not treating people the same way.
unfair.example: It may seem unfair that parents have to invest so much.
